child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: type
child.description: Distinguishes between physical addresses (those you can visit) and mailing addresses (e.g. PO Boxes and care-of addresses). Most...
child.longDesc: Distinguishes between physical addresses (those you can visit) and mailing addresses (e.g. PO Boxes and care-of addresses). Most addresses are both.

child.name: text
child.description: Specifies the entire address as it should be displayed e.g. on a postal label. This may be provided instead of or as well as the...
child.longDesc: Specifies the entire address as it should be displayed e.g. on a postal label. This may be provided instead of or as well as the specific parts.

child.name: line
child.description: This component contains the house number, apartment number, street name, street direction, P.O. Box number, delivery hints, and...
child.longDesc: This component contains the house number, apartment number, street name, street direction, P.O. Box number, delivery hints, and similar address information.

child.name: city
child.description: The name of the city, town, suburb, village or other community or delivery center.

child.name: district
child.description: The name of the administrative area (county).

child.name: state
child.description: Sub-unit of a country with limited sovereignty in a federally organized country. A code may be used if codes are in common use (...
child.longDesc: Sub-unit of a country with limited sovereignty in a federally organized country. A code may be used if codes are in common use (e.g. US 2 letter state codes).

child.name: postalCode
child.description: A postal code designating a region defined by the postal service.

child.name: country
child.description: Country - a nation as commonly understood or generally accepted.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period when address was/is in use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.
